title: Daily Summary — 2026-05-07
body:
## Recent Activity

Synced window starts at `2026-05-06T12:25:01.954Z`. Signals show 3 issues, 4 pull requests, 2 discussions, and 0 commits updated.

Notable activity centered on generated-comment cleanup: [#68](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/68) produced [PR #162](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/162) for collapsing older `fix-pr` status comments, and [#67](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/67) produced [PR #163](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163) for collapsing older same-agent inline review comments. Both PRs reached review synthesis verdicts of `SHIP` with no required branch-change work remaining.

There was also product/design discussion on [PR #133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133), leaning toward thinning the Sepo version CLI unless it is immediately wired into updater or bug-report workflows.

## Recently Active PRs

- [#163](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163) “Collapse older generated inline review comments” is open. It went through several review/fix-pr loops for timestamp source, pagination coverage, best-effort cleanup, and duplicate inline finding behavior; latest synthesis says `SHIP`.
- [#162](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/162) “Collapse old fix-pr status comments” is open. Unsupported status posting was routed through the shared cleanup path; latest synthesis says `SHIP`.
- [#133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133) “Add Sepo version metadata reader CLI” is open. Latest discussion recommends keeping only a minimal workflow helper unless immediately consumed by #116 or #117.
- [#117](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117) “Report failed agent runs to Discussions” is open but appears effectively held; recent project-management activity deprioritized it after the safer approval-gated failure-reporting direction emerged.

## Recently Active Issues

- [#67](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/67) “After a new review, it should also collapse the previous in-line review comments?” triggered orchestration and produced [PR #163](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163).
- [#68](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/68) “For the fix-pr, new fix-pr should collapse previous fix-pr comments?” triggered orchestration and produced [PR #162](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/162).
- [#26](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/26) “Automatically report issues on GitHub” remains open but is now lower priority/effort in the synced signals, reflecting the newer safer failure diagnosis/reporting track.

## Recently Active Discussions

- [#153](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/153) “Add a yolo mode for sepo” discussed an opt-in `agent-self-approve` route gated by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE`. A later `/orchestrate implement this idea` request was rejected because orchestration currently supports issues and PRs only.
- [#161](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/161) “Daily Summary — 2026-05-06” received project-management summaries; the latest pass reported no managed priority/effort label changes needed.

## Follow-ups

- Review/merge [PR #162](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/162) and [PR #163](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/163), since synced review synthesis says both are ready.
- Decide whether [PR #133](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/133) should be thinned now or immediately wired into updater/bug-report workflows.
- Convert discussion [#153](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/153) into an issue if the team wants orchestration to implement the self-approve idea.

---
*Generated by the agent-daily-summary workflow*

nodes.body:
## Project Management Summary

- Mode: `labels applied`
- Open items assessed: `39 issues, 15 pull requests`
- Managed labels: `priority/*`, `effort/*`

### Top Triage Queue

- `issue#126` - What's the best way to manage the tasks and project planning for this repo now? - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - Active parent workstream for Project-backed planning/setup; many staged children and stacked PRs depend on this direction. Label changes: none.
- `issue#156` - Rethink bug resolution, automatic diagnosis, and automatic reporting - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - High-impact UX/reliability redesign connected to failure reporting and PR #158. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#158` - Add approval-gated agent failure diagnosis - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - Implements #156 across runtime failure classification, redaction, artifacts, discussion drafts, docs, and workflow config. Label changes: none.
- `issue#75` - Agent review-body commands can be cancelled by sibling inline review-comment events - `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` - Concrete workflow race that can drop valid user commands; bounded fix with clear acceptance criteria. Label changes: none.
- `issue#119` - App installation automation - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - Important onboarding/distribution capability and linked to PR #120; broad installation bootstrap surface. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#120` - Add GitHub App installation bootstrap - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - Implements installation bootstrap on top of #62 and closes #119; draft status suggests it still needs review/merge work. Label changes: none.
- `issue#74` - Create version systems - `priority/p1`, `effort/high` - Foundational for updates, bug reports, installation identity, and public release readiness. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#133` - Add Sepo version metadata reader CLI - `priority/p1`, `effort/medium` - Current slice under #74; clean draft PR with focused runtime metadata reader. Label changes: none.
- `pull_request#163` - Collapse older generated inline review comments - `priority/p2`, `effort/medium` - Bounded fix for #67 under generated-comment cleanup; clean draft and recently reviewed/fixed. Label changes: add `priority/p2`, `effort/medium`.
- `pull_request#162` - Collapse old fix-pr status comments - `priority/p2`, `effort/low` - Small paired fix for #68 to reduce duplicate generated status comments. Label changes: add `priority/p2`, `effort/low`.

### Label Changes

2 open pull requests are missing managed labels and should receive the selected priority/effort labels. No stale managed labels need removal.

```json
{
  "label_changes": [
    {
      "kind": "pull_request",
      "number": 163,
      "add": ["priority/p2", "effort/medium"],
      "remove": []
    },
    {
      "kind": "pull_request",
      "number": 162,
      "add": ["priority/p2", "effort/low"],
      "remove": []
    }
  ]
}
```

### Notes

Initial `gh issue list` GraphQL call timed out with HTTP 504, so assessment used GitHub REST list endpoints plus targeted PR state GraphQL. No labels, issues, PRs, commits, reviews, or comments were mutated by the agent.

### Managed Label Application

- Applied 4 managed priority/effort label operation(s).
